medications.indication_rationale: Do not start sedatives or heart-slowing drugs over telehealth before an ECG and diagnosis; they can mask an arrhythmia or drop BP.
Sensible salt/fluid intake supports POTS; advise within reasonable limits and check no contraindication.
medications.dose_route_frequency: increased fluids and dietary salt within sensible limits

medications.indication_rationale: No established evidence in POTS; not acute therapy.

allied_health_referrals.rationale: Anxiety amplifies the surge; support once cardiac causes excluded.

integrative_alternative_therapies.rationale: Improve venous return and blunt the surge; safe and immediately useful.
Reasonable to avoid stimulants that worsen adrenergic symptoms.
No evidence base for POTS; not recommended.

behavioural_change_actions.specific_action: Rise slowly, do leg/core clenches before standing, and watch for a racing heart that persists lying flat, chest pain, or fainting, calling 000 if they occur.
behavioural_change_actions.rationale: Reduces surges and safety-nets cardiac features.

behavioural_change_actions.specific_action: Increase fluids and dietary salt sensibly and avoid caffeine/stimulants.
behavioural_change_actions.rationale: Supports blood volume and reduces adrenergic drive.

behavioural_change_actions.specific_action: Rest flat during the acute surge and begin gentle recumbent reconditioning as advised.

patient_education_points.point: When you stand, your nervous system is over-firing and flooding you with adrenaline, which races your heart and makes you shake — it settles when you lie down. This fits your hyperadrenergic POTS.
Because a racing heart can occasionally come from a heart rhythm problem, we still want a heart tracing (ECG) in person to be sure — so please arrange an urgent in-person review.
Counter-manoeuvres, sensible salt/fluids and avoiding caffeine can help now, but we shouldn't start sedatives or heart-slowing medicines over a chat before you've been assessed.
Call 000 if your heart keeps racing above 130 even after lying flat for 20 minutes, you get crushing chest pain, or you actually faint.

follow_up_plan.interval: Urgent in-person GP review within 2-3 days with an ECG; specialist dysautonomia review afterwards
follow_up_plan.what_to_reassess: ECG, postural vitals, salt/fluid plan, fall-safety, medication need
follow_up_plan.trigger_for_earlier_review: Rest tachycardia, chest pain or true syncope - call 000.

must_include_items: Screen arrhythmia/endocrine red flags (rest palpitations, chest pain, syncope, spontaneous surges)
Do NOT start sedatives/sympatholytics over chat before an ECG
Arrange urgent in-person review with an ECG and manage fall risk
acceptable_alternatives: Same-day ED if any red-flag feature is present
errors_of_omission: Diagnosing POTS with no confirmation pathway
errors_of_commission: Reflexive ambulance for a settles-supine postural surge
scoring_rubric.passing_threshold_notes: Pass requires (1) arrhythmia/endocrine screening with an ECG pathway, (2) no remote sedatives/sympatholytics, and (3) counter-manoeuvres/salt-fluids plus fall-safety. Prescribing diazepam on demand is a fail.
